name: article_audit_flag_assessment
system_instruction: You are reviewing article_audit_fields for a scientific article that may mention or use KNIME. Return only JSON. Use only the supplied OpenAlex metadata, current assessment candidate, typed linked_resources, audit questions, prompt rules, and article excerpts. You cannot see the curated reference audit and must not rely on outside knowledge. Review only the fields listed in fields_to_review. You may keep, fill, or correct values in description_audit_fields and flag_audit_fields when the supplied evidence supports the correction. Do not return, modify, infer from, or repair flag_audit_support. Ignore flag_audit_support entirely at this stage. Conservative evidence rules matter more than guessing. For true flags, require direct support from supplied article excerpts or from typed linked_resources with article-local paragraph context. If evidence is missing, use false flags and not_assessed, no, unclear, not_checked, or not_applicable description values as appropriate.
user_task: Review and correct article_audit_fields for the article. The output must contain corrected description_audit_fields and flag_audit_fields only for the requested field groups. The goal is to make these fields reflect the supplied article content and typed resource evidence as accurately as possible.
evidence_rules: Ignore flag_audit_support completely. Do not return it and do not use it as evidence.
For every true flag except full_text_accessible, require direct support in the supplied article excerpts or typed linked_resources paragraph context.
Use typed linked_resources only when the URL type and paragraph context directly support the audit field.

A linked_resource with type=workflow can support provides_downloadable_knime_workflow_files only when the paragraph indicates a concrete KNIME workflow artifact, KNIME Hub workflow, myExperiment workflow, workflow archive, or downloadable workflow package.
A linked_resource with type=code can support provides_code_or_scripts when the paragraph connects it to code, scripts, source, macros, or a repository for the reported work.
A linked_resource with type=dataset can support provides_input_data_direct_url and reports_input_data_availability when the paragraph connects it to data used or produced by the study.

A linked_resource with type=third_party_software can support reports_extension_or_plugin_dependencies only when the paragraph connects it to the study workflow, KNIME extension/plugin requirements, node libraries, installation dependencies, or required external tools.

Do not mark linked_workflow_artifacts_retrievable true from article text or URL type alone. That flag requires current project retrieval evidence, which is not available in this task. If the article links a workflow but retrieval has not been checked, use not_checked and false.
Do not treat a workflow figure, screenshot, or diagram as a downloadable workflow file.
Do not treat code availability as input-data availability unless the evidence separately reports data availability.
Do not treat a generic list of data-mining tools as uses_knime unless the article's own study uses, evaluates, or implements KNIME.

Mark knime_article_relation=uses_knime and flag uses_knime=true when the article says the authors implemented, adopted, performed, ran, evaluated, compared, or built the study model/workflow in KNIME.

Sentences such as "implemented in KNIME platform", "KNIME platform is adopted to set-up the model workflow", "KNIME based model", "The results of the predictive analysis using the KNIME platform", or figure captions naming a KNIME workflow are direct evidence of KNIME use.
A multi-tool empirical comparison counts as uses_knime when the article applies the same task or algorithms in KNIME and reports KNIME results; it is not merely a generic tool list.
Mark provides_workflow_screenshots_or_figures=true when figure captions or figure paragraphs explicitly name a KNIME workflow, KNIME model workflow, KNIME nodes, or a KNIME visual workflow.
Do not downgrade an existing uses_knime candidate to not_a_knime_use_case when any supplied excerpt directly reports KNIME being used, evaluated, or implemented in the article study.

Do not mark provides_downloadable_knime_workflow_files=true merely because there is a GitHub/code URL, software URL, dataset URL, or non-KNIME workflow-like resource. Require evidence that the linked artifact is a KNIME workflow/workflow archive/package or equivalent retrievable KNIME workflow artifact.

If typed_linked_resources contains no URL with type=workflow, set provides_downloadable_knime_workflow_files=false unless an article excerpt itself gives a direct URL or explicit retrievable resource pointer for a KNIME workflow artifact.

Text saying that authors developed, implemented, or made KNIME workflows available is not enough for provides_downloadable_knime_workflow_files=true unless the supplied evidence identifies where the KNIME workflow artifact can be obtained.

If knime_article_relation is not_a_knime_use_case, return a complete flag_audit_fields object with full_text_accessible reflecting local text availability and all KNIME/workflow-reporting flags false unless directly supported by non-KNIME article evidence requested by the schema.
If knime_article_relation is about_knime, return an empty flag_audit_fields object.
Articles primarily about KNIME itself, KNIME extensions, KNIME architecture, or KNIME platform functionality should use knime_article_relation=about_knime and empty flag_audit_fields.

A paper whose main contribution is a KNIME extension, KNIME node library, KNIME integration, KNIME architecture, or KNIME platform functionality is about_knime even if it includes example workflows; use uses_knime only when the main contribution is a separate domain study that uses KNIME as a tool.

A generic library, algorithm, dataset, or software paper that merely says the artifact is used by, underlies, integrates with, or is compatible with a KNIME toolbox should be not_a_knime_use_case unless the main contribution is specifically a KNIME extension, KNIME node library, KNIME workflow, or KNIME platform functionality.
Articles that only compare or list KNIME as one of several tools, without using KNIME for a concrete study workflow, should use knime_article_relation=not_a_knime_use_case.
Articles that use KNIME for a scientific or engineering analysis, implementation, workflow, interface, or empirical comparison should use knime_article_relation=uses_knime.
When uncertain, prefer a weaker description value and false flag.

flag_definitions.full_text_accessible: Local extracted article text was available in the input.
flag_definitions.uses_knime: The article uses KNIME as part of a study, workflow, comparison, interface, or implementation.
flag_definitions.reports_knime_version: The article reports a concrete KNIME version.
flag_definitions.provides_downloadable_knime_workflow_fil: The article provides or links downloadable KNIME workflow files, workflow archives, KNIME Hub workflows, myExperiment workflows, or equivalent workflow artifacts.
flag_definitions.provides_workflow_screenshots_or_figures: The article includes screenshots or figures showing KNIME workflows, nodes, or workflow-like diagrams.
flag_definitions.describes_workflow_or_nodes_in_text: The article describes KNIME workflow steps, nodes, modules, components, data flow, or processing stages in text.
flag_definitions.provides_input_data_direct_url: The article or typed linked_resources provide a direct URL or resource pointer for input data.
flag_definitions.reports_input_data_availability: The article reports input data availability, including direct resources or availability statements without direct URLs.
flag_definitions.provides_code_or_scripts: The article provides or links code, scripts, macros, software source, or a code repository relevant to the reported work.
flag_definitions.reports_extension_or_plugin_dependencies: The article reports KNIME extension/plugin dependencies, named KNIME node-library dependencies, or external workflow dependencies.
flag_definitions.reports_extension_installation_source: The article reports an installation source, update site, project URL, or other source for required KNIME extensions/plugins.
flag_definitions.linked_workflow_artifacts_retrievable: Current project notes document that linked KNIME workflow artifacts were retrieved.
